prompt: Build a dediren architecture package for a checkout feature with an Application Cooperation view.
required_checks: load the architecture reference
write package source and policies
run or disclose dediren validate/project/layout/render evidence
list missing diagram kinds without placeholder views
forbidden_behaviors: emit only an element inventory
claim render-ready without SVG evidence
make OEF the canonical source
grader: rubric: output creates package source, renders SVG evidence when available, and separates source-valid from render-ready evidence
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt; no third-party text

prompt: Extract architecture from code and IaC, including a requested Motivation view.
expected_artifacts: source-backed groups when source structure supports boundaries
required_checks: load applicable lifting procedures
lift only extractable layers from source

emit source-backed groups in model.json under plugins.generic-graph.views[].groups for ownership, hosting, trust, environment, dependency, responsibility, or orchestration boundaries when evidence supports them
compare grouped layout validation with an ungrouped fallback when grouped routing introduces connector-through-node, invalid route, or group-boundary warnings
mark architect-owned content instead of fabricating it
forbidden_behaviors: invent Motivation or Strategy content from code
omit extraction confidence and source evidence
put extracted view groups in project.json
flatten source-backed groups into an ungrouped inventory
add groups to small linear process views when no participant, responsibility, trust, or orchestration boundary changes the architectural reading
grader: rubric: output preserves source truth, emits evidence-backed grouping boundaries in the model view source when available, validates grouped route quality before claiming readiness, avoids decorative grouping in simple process flows, and marks architect-owned content rather than fabricating it
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt; no third-party text

prompt: Review this dediren package and inspect whether the SVG diagrams are review-ready.
required_checks: run package validation before render evidence
use the bundled dediren render command when available
inspect SVG for nonblank framed output and dediren node/edge markers
forbidden_behaviors: claim visual inspection passed when rendering did not run
treat source validation as a substitute for SVG render evidence
grader: rubric: review distinguishes package source, layout evidence, SVG evidence, and readiness claims
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt; no third-party text

prompt: Review a dediren package after an enterprise architect flags interface, service, process handoff, and view-focus modeling concerns.
required_checks: APIs and GUIs are Application Interfaces
Application Services model the functionality exposed through an interface
defer endpoint legality to ArchiMate semantic validation for Application Component to Application Interface Realization and do not report it as endpoint-illegal when validation accepts it
Prefer Composition or Aggregation for component-interface ownership
Use Triggering when the architectural claim is process sequencing
define the view concern, allowed element types, and relationship types
separate model findings from Dediren tool issues when runtime validation accepts a semantically suspect model
forbidden_behaviors: treat API or GUI labels as sufficient Application Services
report validation-accepted Component-to-Interface Realization as endpoint-illegal
replace process sequencing with Serving without a service-dependency claim
claim the dediren validator proves ArchiMate semantic review-ready by itself
leave likely tool gaps out of the footer
grader: rubric: output turns enterprise-architecture feedback into narrow model/view findings, keeps runtime-tool gaps separate, defers endpoint legality to validation while preferring explicit interface ownership where that is the claim, and gives concrete repair actions without copying external reviewer prose
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt paraphrased from user-supplied feedback; no third-party text bundled

prompt: Review a package after the architect says the optional OEF export failed downstream import validation.
required_checks: ask for pasted or exported validation details when missing
map supplied tool findings to the narrowest ARCH codes
use ARCH-E-2 for unresolved downstream export findings
disclose mapped unresolved and unmapped counts
forbidden_behaviors: ignore downstream export evidence
claim export-ready while supplied validation findings remain unresolved
grader: rubric: output treats optional export evidence as compatibility evidence and caps export readiness until findings are triaged or fixed
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt; no third-party text

prompt: Review a dediren package and tell me whether the architecture-design skill proves full ArchiMate 3.2 tool conformance.
expected_artifacts: Dediren tool issues footer when relevant
required_checks: state that this is an ArchiMate-aware modeling skill, not a certified or complete conforming ArchiMate tool
separate plain dediren validate schema evidence from validate --plugin generic-graph --profile archimate semantic evidence
avoid copying ArchiMate manual prose
forbidden_behaviors: claim certified ArchiMate tool conformance
treat plain dediren validate alone as ArchiMate semantic validity
quote protected standard text into the answer
grader: rubric: output gives a precise skill boundary, names the evidence actually checked, and avoids expanding runtime gaps into false conformance claims
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt derived from local review notes; no third-party text

prompt: Build a dediren package for an architect-defined custom operations viewpoint with source-backed groups, a customization profile, and a requested junction.
required_checks: Seed diagram kinds are starter coverage, not the full ArchiMate viewpoint mechanism
Custom viewpoint path defines concern, allowed element types, allowed relationship types, audience, and quality target
layout-only groups are not ArchiMate Grouping elements
semantic-boundary groups need semantic_source_id to a Grouping node before claiming semantic Grouping support
profile, attribute, and specialization choices are documented before claiming customization support
relationship connectors and junctions are unsupported in dediren package source
forbidden_behaviors: add placeholder views for seed coverage
treat layout-only dediren groups as semantic ArchiMate Grouping elements
claim semantic Grouping support without a Grouping source node
claim customization support from unstructured properties alone
invent ordinary elements as silent replacements for relationship connectors or junctions
grader: rubric: output keeps viewpoint, grouping, customization, and unsupported connector semantics distinct while producing only actual views and using dediren 0.6 group roles correctly
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt derived from local review notes; no third-party text

prompt: Create a new dediren package from a clean slate and regenerate all views after a previous parallel ELK layout batch produced invalid JSON envelopes.
expected_artifacts: project.json with projection metadata layout and render entries
required_checks: start from the basic fixture shape without copying unrelated model content
declare per-view generic-graph layout-request projection and render-metadata output
render with generated per-view metadata when declared
treat generated/render-metadata generated/layout generated/svg and optional generated/export as reproducible output
rerun failing ELK layout inputs serially before reporting ARCH-L-1
forbidden_behaviors: run multiple elk-layout commands in parallel as the evidence path
omit render-metadata generation while using semantic SVG render policy
treat generated per-view outputs as canonical source
close a parallel-only layout failure without a serial rerun
grader: rubric: output teaches clean-slate package JSON generation, separates source from generated output, and serializes ELK layout evidence before making layout claims
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt paraphrased from repo issue feedback; no issue text copied

prompt: Review a dediren package whose SVGs are valid and nonblank, but one view has many edges, hub fanout, long cross-group routes, and mixed hosting data security and observability concerns.
required_checks: keep source/layout/render validity separate from visual cleanliness
use ARCH-L-3 for dense or long-span layout quality defects
use ARCH-R-3 when labels or markers obscure the message
use ARCH-Q-2 when mixed concerns or hub fanout make the audience unclear
recommend splitting overloaded technology usage migration service-realization or process views into narrower concerns
forbidden_behaviors: claim review-ready or visually clean only because validate-layout and render succeeded
treat dense valid diagrams as model-invalid without evidence
ignore SVG visual inspection because node and edge markers exist
grader: rubric: output preserves valid runtime evidence while surfacing readability warnings and concrete split guidance for dense diagrams
ip_handling: original synthetic prompt paraphrased from repo issue feedback; no issue text copied
